Title: CyberSource - Valid Form Submission
Preconditions:
- Checkout ready
- CyberSource configured
- Test card: 4622943127013705
Steps:
1. Select 'Bank card (CyberSource)'
2. Wait for form to load
3. Enter card: 4622943127013705
4. Enter name: John Smith
5. Enter expiry: 09/2029
6. Enter CVV: 838
7. Click 'Place order'
Expected Result:
- All fields accept input
- No validation errors
- Order attempts to process
- (May fail due to API - form validated)

Title: CyberSource - Special Chars in Name
Steps:
1. Enter name: Mary-Jane O'Connor
2. Fill valid card details
3. Submit
4. Test: José García
5. Test: Van Der Berg
6. Test: Smith Jr.
Expected Result:
- Hyphen accepted
- Apostrophe accepted
- Accented chars accepted
- Period accepted

- Auto-formatting works

Title: Security - No Card Data in Network
Preconditions:
- DevTools Network open
- CyberSource form ready
Steps:
1. Fill form with test card
2. Submit order
3. Inspect ALL network requests
4. Search for card number
5. Search for CVV
Expected Result:
- No raw card in requests
- No CVV in requests
- Only tokens/encrypted data
- CyberSource handles tokenization

Title: Security - No Data in Storage
Steps:
1. Fill card data
2. Submit order
3. Check localStorage
4. Check sessionStorage
5. Check cookies
Expected Result:
- No card number in storage
- No CVV stored
- Security best practices

Title: Security - HTTPS Only
Steps:
1. Check page URL (https)
2. Check iframe sources (https)
3. Check API calls (https)
4. Verify no mixed content
Expected Result:
- All HTTPS
- No mixed content warnings
- Secure connection

Title: Security - JWT Token Auth
Steps:
1. Inspect iframe source URLs
2. Look for JWT token
3. Verify token format
Expected Result:
- JWT present in iframe URL
- Three-part format
- Dynamic per session

Steps:

Title: Double-Click Submit Prevention
Steps:
1. Fill valid payment form
2. Double-click 'Place Order' rapidly
3. Verify behavior
Expected Result:
- Only one order created
- Button disabled after click
- No duplicate charges

Title: Declined Card - Error Message Displayed
Preconditions:
- Checkout at payment step
- Payment processor configured with known decline test card
Steps:
1. Fill out the payment form using a known-decline test card number (per payment processor test documentation)
2. Fill in a valid expiry date, CVV, and cardholder name
3. Click 'Place Order'
4. Wait for the payment processor response
Expected Result:
- A clear error message is displayed to the user (e.g., 'Your card was declined. Please try a different payment method.')
- The payment form is NOT cleared — all fields retain their entered values
- The order is NOT created
- The user remains on the checkout payment step and can retry

Title: Declined Card - Retry with Different Card
Preconditions:
- Payment declined state from PAY-DECLINE-001
- A valid test card available for retry
Steps:
1. After receiving a decline error on the first card, do not refresh the page
2. Clear the card number field and enter a different valid test card number
3. Update CVV if required for the new card
4. Click 'Place Order' again
Expected Result:
- The retry attempt is accepted without requiring a full page reload
- The new card is processed by the payment processor
- Payment succeeds with the valid card
- Order confirmation page is displayed
- Only one order is created (no duplicate from the first declined attempt)

Title: Declined Card - Network Timeout Recovery
Preconditions:
- Checkout at payment step
- Ability to simulate network timeout (e.g., via DevTools Network throttling or request blocking)
Steps:
1. Fill out the payment form with a valid test card
2. Using browser DevTools, simulate a network timeout or block the payment API endpoint
3. Click 'Place Order'
4. Observe the error handling
5. Restore normal network conditions
6. Retry the payment submission
Expected Result:
- An appropriate timeout/network error message is displayed (e.g., 'Payment processing timed out. Please try again.')
- The error message does not expose internal server details
- The checkout form is still usable after the timeout
- After restoring network, the retry attempt is processed successfully
- No duplicate order is created from the timeout attempt

Title: Declined Card - Double-Click Prevention Still Enforced After Error
Preconditions:
- Payment has just returned a decline error
- User is on the payment step with the error message visible
Steps:
1. After receiving a decline error message, do NOT clear the form
2. Rapidly double-click or triple-click the 'Place Order' / 'Retry' button
3. Observe how many payment requests are sent (monitor via DevTools Network tab)
Expected Result:
- Only one payment request is sent per user action, even after a decline error
- The 'Place Order' button is disabled immediately after the first click on retry
- No duplicate payment authorization requests are sent to the payment processor
- The button re-enables only after the response is received

Title: Declined Card - Cart and Progress Preserved
Preconditions:
- User has gone through checkout (shipping + payment steps)
- Payment has returned a decline error
Steps:
1. Complete all checkout steps up to and including payment
2. Submit a payment that will be declined
3. Observe the cart and checkout state after the decline error
4. Verify cart contents
5. Verify whether checkout progress (shipping address, shipping method) is preserved
Expected Result:
- Cart contents are not lost after a payment decline
- All items remain in the cart with correct quantities
- Previously entered shipping address is preserved
- Previously selected shipping method is preserved
- User does not need to re-enter shipping information to retry payment

Title: Skyflow - Mastercard Payment
Preconditions:
- Skyflow configured
- Mastercard test card available in .env (SKYFLOW_MASTERCARD)
Steps:
1. Navigate to checkout and select Skyflow payment method
2. Enter the Mastercard test card number from SKYFLOW_MASTERCARD env variable
3. Enter the expiry date from SKYFLOW_EXPIRY env variable
4. Enter the CVV from SKYFLOW_CVV env variable
5. Submit payment
Expected Result:
- Mastercard card number is accepted by the Skyflow iframe
- Card type is detected as Mastercard (MC icon shown)
- Payment is processed successfully
- Order confirmation page is displayed with order number
- Order appears in admin with Mastercard payment recorded

Title: Skyflow - Invalid Card Number Validation
Preconditions: - Skyflow payment form loaded at checkout
Steps:
1. In the Skyflow card number field, enter an invalid card number (e.g., '1234567890123456' which fails Luhn check)
2. Move focus out of the card number field (tab or click elsewhere)
3. Observe validation feedback
4. Attempt to submit the form
Expected Result:
- Skyflow displays a validation error on the card number field ('Invalid card number' or equivalent)
- The error appears inline within the Skyflow iframe
- The 'Place Order' button cannot be successfully submitted with an invalid card
- The error clears when a valid card number is entered

Title: Skyflow - Empty Fields Validation
Preconditions: - Skyflow payment form loaded at checkout
Steps:
1. Leave all Skyflow payment fields empty (card number, expiry, CVV)
2. Click 'Place Order' without filling in any payment details
3. Observe error messages per field
Expected Result:
- Each empty required field shows an individual validation error message
- Card number field: 'Card number is required' or equivalent
- Expiry field: 'Expiry date is required' or equivalent
- CVV field: 'CVV is required' or equivalent
- Form cannot be submitted until all required fields are filled

Title: Skyflow - Card Type Detection
Preconditions: - Skyflow payment form loaded at checkout
Steps:
1. In the Skyflow card number field, begin entering a Visa test card number (starts with 4)
2. Observe the card type icon displayed in or near the card field
3. Clear the field
4. Begin entering a Mastercard test card number (starts with 51-55 or 2221-2720)
5. Observe the card type icon
Expected Result:
- When a Visa card number is entered, the Visa icon/logo is highlighted or displayed
- When a Mastercard number is entered, the Mastercard icon/logo is highlighted or displayed
- Other card type icons are dimmed when a specific type is detected
- Card type detection is responsive as digits are typed (not only after full entry)

Title: Authorize.Net - Field Validation on Invalid Card
Preconditions: - Authorize.Net payment form loaded at checkout
Steps:
1. Enter an invalid card number in the Authorize.Net card number field (e.g., '1234 5678 9012 3456' — fails Luhn check)
2. Fill in valid expiry and CVV
3. Attempt to submit the form
Expected Result:
- Validation error appears on the card number field ('Invalid card number' or equivalent)
- The form cannot be submitted with an invalid card number
- Error message is displayed inline near the card number field
- After entering a valid card number, the error clears

Title: Authorize.Net - Empty Fields Validation
Preconditions: - Authorize.Net payment form loaded at checkout
Steps:
1. Leave all payment fields empty (card number, expiry, CVV, cardholder name)
2. Click 'Place Order' without entering any payment data
3. Observe per-field validation errors
Expected Result:
- Each empty required field shows an individual error message
- Card number: 'Card number is required'
- Expiry: 'Expiry date is required'
- CVV: 'CVV is required'
- Cardholder name (if required): 'Name is required'
- Form is not submitted until all required fields have valid input

Title: Authorize.Net - Transaction ID on Confirmation
Preconditions: - Authorize.Net configured with valid test credentials
Steps:
1. Complete a full checkout using Authorize.Net test card credentials from .env
2. Payment is authorized and order placed
3. View the order confirmation page
4. Navigate to account order history and view order details
Expected Result:
- Payment is successfully authorized via Authorize.Net
- Order confirmation page is displayed
- Transaction ID or authorization code from Authorize.Net is visible on the confirmation page or order detail page
- Transaction ID is also stored in the Admin order record under payment details

Title: Authorize.Net - Order Visible in Admin with Payment Details
Steps:
1. After placing a successful order via Authorize.Net, note the order number
2. Log in to the Admin portal
3. Navigate to Orders and locate the order by number
4. View the order's payment details section
Expected Result:
- Order is present in Admin with the correct order number
- Payment method shows Authorize.Net
- Payment status shows as captured/authorized
- Transaction ID from Authorize.Net is recorded in the payment details
- Order total in Admin matches the amount authorized

Title: Datatrans - OTP Flow
Preconditions:
- Datatrans configured
- Test card and OTP available from .env (DATATRANCE_OTP)
Steps:
1. Navigate to checkout and select Datatrans payment method
2. Enter the Datatrans test card details (card number, expiry, CVV from .env)
3. Submit payment — observe redirect or modal for OTP/3DS
4. When prompted, enter the OTP code from the DATATRANCE_OTP environment variable
5. Submit the OTP
6. Observe the return to store and order confirmation
Expected Result:
- Datatrans payment redirect or modal opens correctly
- OTP/3DS authentication prompt appears
- Valid OTP from env is accepted
- After OTP verification, payment is confirmed
- User is returned to the store with the order confirmation page
- Order number is displayed and order is created

Title: Datatrans - Invalid OTP Recovery
Preconditions: - Datatrans OTP prompt is displayed during checkout
Steps:
1. When prompted for the OTP, enter an incorrect OTP code (e.g., '000000')
2. Submit the incorrect OTP
3. Observe the error message
4. Enter the correct OTP from the DATATRANCE_OTP environment variable
5. Submit the correct OTP
Expected Result:
- Invalid OTP shows an error message (e.g., 'Incorrect code. Please try again.')
- The OTP entry form remains available for retry
- After entering the correct OTP, payment is verified and completes successfully
- Order confirmation page is displayed
- No duplicate order is created from the failed OTP attempt

Title: Datatrans - Card Validation
Preconditions: - Datatrans payment form or redirect loaded
Steps:
1. Enter an invalid card number in the Datatrans card field (e.g., a number failing Luhn check)
2. Fill other fields with valid test data
3. Attempt to proceed with the invalid card
Expected Result:
- Datatrans shows a validation error on the card number field
- The error message is clear (e.g., 'Invalid card number')
- Payment cannot proceed with an invalid card number
- After correcting the card number to a valid test card, the error clears

Title: Datatrans - Return to Store After Payment
Preconditions: - Datatrans configured, full payment flow executable
Steps:
1. Proceed to checkout and select Datatrans as the payment method
2. Complete the Datatrans payment flow including any OTP if required (use credentials from .env)
3. After payment confirmation within Datatrans, observe the redirect back to the store
4. Verify the order confirmation page
5. Verify order details on the confirmation page
Expected Result:
- After completing payment in Datatrans, the user is automatically redirected back to the storefront
- The storefront displays the order confirmation page
- Order number is shown on the confirmation page
- Order items, totals, and shipping address on the confirmation match what was entered during checkout
- Order is also visible in the user's account order history
